pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take: **Look, I get why people say the World Cup is unpredictable, but when you draft three teams that didn’t even qualify, bless your heart, you might want to rethink your strategy, Noisemaker.** Rafe finds himself in a respectable fourth place, tied with both Bubba G and Ghost Pepper, but let's be real, he's stumbled into this position thanks to his teams' good work rather than any masterminding on his part. Meanwhile, "Ghost Pepper" Devin is clinging desperately to second place, but that fourth-round pick of Haiti is a decision that had me scratching my head even though I feel for him in his quest for the underdog story. I mean, what conversation was he having with himself? His recent win with the USA isn't a saving grace; it's a temporary band-aid on a self-inflicted wound. As for "Mustard Boy" Zach, currently leading with the slimmest of margins at the top, it's probably a case of being in the right place at the right time with teams like Brazil doing much of the heavy lifting. Meanwhile, "The Backpass Assassin" Gayden, sitting in the lonely bottom with 10 points, might want to reassess his whole tactical approach unless his dream is to be lauded for participation.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach Holds Top Spot, Credits Saudi Arabia Draft Strategy
pundit_takes.subtitle: Mustard Boy's Saudi gamble pays zero dividends, but he's happy.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden's Draft Tactics Questioned After Dropping to Last Place
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Backpass Assassin's teams yet to perform any forward passes.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe Expected to Lose Ground as Germany Faces Ivory Coast
pundit_takes.subtitle: The Noisemaker confident his draft of non-qualifiers remains solid.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner and Devin in Fierce Battle for Second Place Supremacy
pundit_takes.subtitle: Bubba G congratulates Ghost Pepper on scoring easier group points.

rome_analytics_quotes: Zach's the king with no points today. Leadership on autopilot, luck or skill?
Rafe's Tier-1 Germany can't save his Tier-2 dumpster fire picks.
Rafe owns Devin like Ghost Pepper owns a winning streak: not at all.
The sim's 15-year-old chaos agent is jamming up Gunner's top-dog predictions.
